title: Ma famille — Family & possessives
objective: Name family members and say what belongs to you using possessives (A1: can describe family and immediate surroundings).

bridge.title: Bridge from Hindi / English: possessives agree with the THING, not the owner
bridge.body: In French, **mon** / **ma** match the gender of the noun owned, not the speaker: a girl still says **mon livre** (my book, masc.) and **ma sœur** (my sister, fem.). English “his/her” works the opposite way. Good news — Hindi already agrees like French: मेरा भाई (mera bhai, masc.) vs मेरी बहन (meri behen, fem.). Lean on that instinct. ⚠️ Note: **un parent** often means “a relative”, not only “a parent”.

quiz.prompt: What is the main bridge idea for "Ma famille — Family & possessives"?
options: Focus on: possessives agree with the THING, not the owner

example.french: Pendant Diwali, ma famille se réunit à Jaipur.
example.englishGloss: During Diwali, my family gathers in Jaipur.

glosses.en: During Diwali, my family gathers in Jaipur.
glosses.ta: தீபாவளி போது, என் குடும்பம் Jaipur-இல் கூடுகிறது.
glosses.te: దీపావళి సమయంలో, నా కుటుంబం Jaipur-లో కలుస్తుంది.
glosses.kn: ದೀಪಾವಳಿ ಸಮಯದಲ್ಲಿ, ನನ್ನ ಕುಟುಂಬ Jaipur-ನಲ್ಲಿ ಸೇರುತ್ತದೆ.

exercises.prompt: What does « J'ai deux frères » mean?
exercises.explanation: « J'ai » = I have (avoir). « deux frères » = two brothers.
